title: Ends of strings should be checked with startsWith() and endsWith()
description: Ends of strings should be checked with "startsWith()" and "endsWith()"Analyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEtype-dependentWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoWhen writing code, it is quite common to test patterns against string ends. For a long time, JavaScript did not provide proper support for this use case. As a result, developers have been relying on various programming subtleties to check the start or end of a string.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Use startsWith() and endsWith() methods instead of indexOf/substring comparisons
